name: Eerie Witch's Brew
recipeIngredient: 1 can (12 oz) Thawed frozen apple-juice concentrate
1 can (6 oz) Thawed frozen limeade concentrate
1 lb Dry ice - up to 2 lbs
recipeInstructions:
A smoking cauldron of punch made with grapes and orange peel masquerading as eyeballs and worms.
A SERIOUS CAUTION: Never touch dry ice; use tongs to handle.
In a 1 to 2 quart pan, bring 1 cup of cranberry juice and candied ginger to a boil over high heat. Boil, uncovered, about 2 minutes, then set aside.
With a vegetable peeler, pare the colored part (peel) from oranges; cut peel into thin 2-inch-long worms or use an Oriental shredder to make long shreds. Add orange peel to cranberry mixture. Cover and chill for at least 4 hours or as long as overnight.
Juice the oranges; put juice in a 6 to 8 quart pan or heavy bowl. Stir in the cranberry-ginger mixture, the 3 cups cranberry juice, apple concentrate, limeade, grapes, and water. If made ahead, cover and chill for up to 2 hours. Add ginger ale and about a 1 pound piece of dry ice (do not add small pieces to punch or cups); ice should smolder for at least 30 minutes. Ladle into cups. Add any remaining ice when bubbling ceases.
Makes 5 quarts; allows for approximately 1-1/2 cups per serving.
